sections.paragraph:
περιχαρής, ές (χαίρω; Soph., Hdt. et al.; 3 Macc 5:44; TestSol; Philo, Rer. Div. Her. 3; Jos., Ant. 7, 206) very glad π. γενέσθαι (Diod S 20, 76, 6 π. γενόμενος; Chariton 6, 5, 1; Ael. Aristid. 50, 50 K.=26 p. 517 D.; Job 3:22; 29:22; TestSol 1:8; Jos., Ant. 1, 284; 16, 358; PSI 887, 5 [VI a.d.]) Hv 3, 12, 2; foll. by the inf. in the gen. v 3, 8, 1.—DELG s.v. χαίρω IB.

Soph Soph , V b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Diod Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Chariton Chariton , scriptor eroticus (writer of romances) I–II a.d.—List 5

Ael. Aristid. Ael. Aristid. = Aelius Aristides, II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

PSI PSI = Papiri greci e latini: Pubblicazioni della Società Italiana—List 4

Hv Hv = Visions

foll. foll. = followed, following

inf. inf. = infinitive

gen. gen. = genitive

v v = Visions

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 808
